section_heading: VFDP-15 -Approval for modified VITA-FAST experiment plan 2025

text:

totalling $135K for the development of new chemical entities (NCE's). This plan and budget scoped out the ordering and testing of two rounds of NCE's (round 2 and round 3) as well as ancillary and confirmatory experiments in worms and human aging cellines.
Following the 2nd round of NCE testing and target discovery experiments at Newcastle we found that we the structural modifications tested in the 2 round of NCE's were not binding these targets any more potently than those in the first round. Hence we have two important pieces of information on the basis of which we are modifying the plan.(1) We now know the 1st, and have a strong hypothesis on the 2nd, know that the chemical moieties tested in round 2 do not contribute to the target binding.

Objective To use the target information to develop the VITA-FAST compounds to a lead candidate, with a target identified and understanding of mechanistic basis of the compounds healthspan and longevity effects on human cells. These experiments should allow us to define our lead molecule(s) for subsequent IND enabling activities such as murine safety experiments. We will also file IP for these compounds.
This plan is designed to allow the VITA-FAST project to hit project milestones (1) Lead compound, (2) Target and towards humans.
PlanCancellation of previously planned experiments-We propose to cancel the otherexperiments that were approved in VFDP-13 that have not yet been conducted, specifically that is (1) Worm longevity at Ora SAR NCE Synthesis. To note - we are cancelling these experiments for now, they are not bad experiments just not appropriate given our current information, in the future any or all of these may again become useful with the compounds we continue to develop. To date we have spent $56,620 of the $135K budgeted in VFDP-13 and now we are replanning the next experiments.

New Experiments Plan The new plan we propose is: (1) Conduct confirmatory experiments to confirm Buq abie <> np op uo em jey mou punoo suod Ayi
After these are complete we will most likely want to order this computationally optimised compound as an NCE for lab testing however we will update the plan at that time.
Cost Implications The cost of VFDP-13 was $135K, of which we spent $56,620 in line with the budget items. The next steps of the experimental plan can be conducted within the existing resources at the University of Newcastle and with our medicinal chemist subcontractor Dr Johannes Reynisson, Lecturer in Drug Discovery at the University of Keele (originally added to the project in VFDP-4). Accordingly we seek budget approval for:
Additional siRNA reagents ordered @Newcastle, Cost = $3K. Additional siRNA reagents ordered @Newcastle, Months = Aug-Dec. Extension of MedChem subcontract, Cost = $8K. Extension of MedChem subcontract, Months = Jul-Dec
allocated but unrealised costs) and hence is sufficient to fund this plan and have funding for the follow on experiments resulting from this data.

Recommendation VITA-FAST has developed potent and novel compounds -this proposal seeks to leverage computational development of those molecules at a highly efficient price point utilising the improving the VF series compounds to generate value in the VITA-FAST project and raise further funding.
